amatrix 0.1.0

  • Changelog

Create a backend-aware sparse matrix

Source: R/constructors.R
adgCMatrix.Rd

Converts a sparse or dense matrix to an adgCMatrix with the specified backend, policy, and precision. This is the primary user-facing constructor for sparse amatrix objects.

Usage

adgCMatrix(
  x,
  mode = NULL,
  backend = NULL,
  preferred_backend = NULL,
  policy = NULL,
  precision = NULL
)

Arguments

x

A dgCMatrix, other sparseMatrix, or base R matrix.

mode

Single string shortcut passed to .amatrix_resolve_mode(). Pass NULL to use the individual arguments. mode = "fast" prefers an available fast-capable accelerator automatically, with CPU fallback.

backend

Alias for preferred_backend; ignored when preferred_backend is non-NULL.

preferred_backend

Single string naming the preferred compute backend.

policy

Single string; one of "auto", "cpu", "mlx", "metal", "arrayfire", "opencl".

precision

Single string; "strict" or "fast".

Value

An adgCMatrix with the data from x and the requested backend metadata.

Examples

m <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 2), nrow = 2)
S <- adgCMatrix(m)
S
#> An amatrix sparse matrix [cpu|policy=auto|precision=strict]
#> 2 x 2 sparse Matrix of class "adgCMatrix"
#>         
#> [1,] 1 .
#> [2,] . 2
